places.name: Kamakhya
places.modernInfo: Most powerful Shakti Peetha. Ambubachi Mela held annually. The goddess has no idol — a natural cleft in rock is worshipped.

scenes.title: Sati's womb falls here
scenes.description: When Vishnu dismembered Sati's body to stop Shiva's grief, 51 body parts fell across the subcontinent. Kamakhya is where Sati's womb fell — making it the most sacred of all Shakti Peethas.

scenes.title: The Goddess bleeds
scenes.description: Every year in June the goddess menstruates — the Brahmaputra river runs red for three days. The temple closes for Ambubachi. Devotees receive red cloth as prasad — one of the most powerful blessings.

places.name: Vaishno Devi
places.modernInfo: 8 million pilgrims annually — one of India's most visited shrines. 14km trek through Trikuta mountains at 5200 feet.

scenes.title: The goddess meditates in the cave
scenes.description: Vaishno Devi meditated in this cave for 9 months with the demon Bhairavnath pursuing her. She finally revealed her full divine form and destroyed him — his head flew to the mountain peak nearby.

places.name: Kalighat
places.modernInfo: Sati's right toe fell here. Kolkata's name derives from Kalighat. Ramakrishna Paramahamsa worshipped here daily.

scenes.title: Kali appears at Kalighat
scenes.description: Sati's right big toe fell at this spot on the banks of the Adi Ganga. The goddess Kali — the fierce, primordial form — has been worshipped here since time immemorial.

places.name: Mahalakshmi — Kolhapur
places.modernInfo: Sati's eyes fell here. The goddess here faces west — unique among all Shakti Peethas. The sunrise directly illuminates the idol only on the 3 days around the equinoxes.

scenes.title: Lakshmi destroys Kolhasura
scenes.description: The demon Kolhasura's terror was ended by Mahalakshmi who appeared here and slew him. Before dying, he requested the city be named after him — she granted it. Kolhapur means 'city of Kolha'.

places.name: Kanyakumari Devi
places.modernInfo: Southernmost tip of India. Three oceans meet here — Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal, Indian Ocean. Sati's back fell here. Vivekananda Rock memorial visible offshore.

scenes.title: The virgin goddess waits
scenes.description: The goddess waited here as a virgin bride for Shiva who never came — he was distracted. She remains Kanya (virgin) Kumari (goddess) forever, her nose ring so bright it misleads sailors into thinking it is a lighthouse.
